# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js Express application. Use the official Node.js base image and install dependencies with npm install. The app listens on port 3000. This is for our microservices architecture that needs rapid deployment cycles.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:14-alpine as builder # create a new stage in the Docker image, use NodeJS 14 and Alpine Linux for size optimization (it's lighter than traditional base images)
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./   # copy all files from current directory to working dir of container. This is where npm install will take place in the next step, before copying source code into image — this allows us not only for security but also speed up build process by avoiding unnecessary dependencies installation during runtime (it's a common practice when building Docker images)
RUN npm install   # run 'npm install', which is equivalent to running `node_modules/.bin/npm-cli.js install` in the context of our dockerfile, installing all packages listed inside package*.json file into container’s node modules folder (this step will be skipped if there are no changes)
COPY . ./   # copy source code from current directory to working dir on image build stage — this is where we'll provide the actual application for Docker. It can also include any other files or directories that your app needs, such as static assets and environment variables (if needed). This step will be skipped if there are no changes
EXPOSE 3000   # expose port to connect from outside of container - in this case it's exposed on the host machine’s network interface. It can also change depending upon your needs, e.g., you might want only certain ports opened or all but not others (in which cases use CMD instead)
CMD [ "node", "server.js" ]   # set default command to run when container starts — in this case it's running the server file located at root of your project, using NodeJS runtime environment and listening on port defined by EXPOSE instruction (this step will be skipped if there are no changes)
